# /fdd

> Assemble or extend the project's **domain** reporting FDD (per [ADR-0006](../../../design/adr/0006-doc-scope-domain-vs-feature.md)). Each new report / dataset adds a feature-tagged structural section + rows in the report catalogue.

## Usage

```
/fdd [--feature <slug>] [--project <name>]
```

## Inputs

- `projects/{p}/reporting/features/{f}/spec.md` (`SPEC_APPROVED` APPROVED)
- [templates/fdd.template.md](../../templates/fdd.template.md)
- `project.config.yaml reporting.*`

## Execution flow

1. Resolve target: `projects/{p}/reporting/fdd.md`. Bootstrap from template if absent.
2. For the current feature, additive merge:
   - **Report catalogue** rows (one per report / dashboard / dataset) — name, audience, refresh, sensitivity label, RLS coverage, FR mapping
   - **Per-report structural sections** — visuals, drill-throughs, KPI definitions
   - **Dataset catalogue** rows — model, tables, refresh window, gateway
   - **Dataflow catalogue** rows — source, schedule, partition strategy
   - **NFR mapping** per feature (refresh-time targets, dataset-size targets)
3. **Inline self-check** against [templates/checklists/fdd-review.checklist.md](../../templates/checklists/fdd-review.checklist.md).
4. Run `doc_lint`.
5. `currentStates += FDD_DRAFT`.

## Output

- `projects/{p}/reporting/fdd.md` (additive)

- [constitution/01-ce-ssrs-standards.md](../../constitution/01-ce-ssrs-standards.md)
